item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Series 1 already had a reanimating rain, and this is not different enough to make me upvote, altough it is not bad enough for a downvote.
No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im gonna echo a sentiment shared above: I was ready to upvote until the final personal report. Too melodramatic and tales-y. It took the clinical nature out of it and was rather superfluous and distracting. It was quite enjoyable until then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not quite sure, but is the myth related to the mysterious lights seen in Blue Ridge Mountains? Mostly seen in Brown Mountain? It was sung in a song that have been remade by different artists that these lights were the spirits/the dead heading back to their masters, or kin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The addendum is quite refreshing, puts on the perspective that Foundation is also people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Affect is not effect, even though the use of the former is the one you affected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, I hadn't read that one- or possibly a long time ago, but I didn't remember it. In any case I believe the two are sufficiently distinct to invoke the "Moving Statue" clause, but in any case, thanks for bringing that to my attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is already a thing you guys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this until I got to the Auxiliary bits. While I wouldn't say they ruined it, I just didn't care for them, and they felt like they somehow detracted from it. Can't quite put my finger on what it is, though.

I would like to stress that I am not referring to the section that begins, "In 2006, 100 Foundation operatives…" I like that part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Note- I added another addendum for the hell of it (though I decided I liked the word "Auxillary" better then addendum.) I don't think it clears much up, but still might do a bit more with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks guys, I rewrote and reorganized a little bit, and plan to do more later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like it, but there are part that are a bit confusing, I will not upvote untill I get a clearer image of the SCP. Also what are does XX-X suppose to mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept feels very solid, but the writeup could use some work. The addendum comes across as overly melodramatic, and still includes what look like placeholder X's. Also, you mention "square miles" - this is not a Metric unit. Fix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely believe the original myth, whatever it is, is a solid basis for an SCP. That being said, I feel like the execution was off. Tonally, this feels too much like a tale and not enough like an SCP. It also really just didn't grab me that much; I felt like some of the details were a bit extraneous, like the whole "bodies disapearing when the ghosts disappear" thing. I don't know, this one just didn't really work for me.

Also, you left in a few XX's throughout, and I got really confused with some of the phrasing in the containment procedures and early on in the description. I get that you were trying to keep us in the dark about what the SCP actually was until about half way down the page, but the result was that you used so much vague wording and pronouns that I completely lost track of what I was reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat:

I don't get what legend this is referring to, but I like this. It has a lot of neat little details. Though… the fact that I see all these neat little details makes me wonder if you could push this more. Add even more interesting stuff. It does feel slightly bare-bones. Not that I think it needs to get real bloated, or even much longer.

I love the example of the ghostly hound. I love that all the entities do is try to rejoin their species. I like the oddness of the original corpse vanishing. I like that they aren't malevolent… so far as we know. Just lonely and transient.

And, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, before anyone asks-
No, I'm not going to tell you what the origin myth is (unless you guess it directly.) In any case, I figured the contest theme wasn't specific, and since mythology is specifically in the SCP, it works with or without the origin myth.
